title: Synthesis 13
visual_description: A dynamic shader effect featuring a dark navy-black background (#0a0f12) with two prominent sine wave patterns creating an organic, flowing composition. The primary visual element is a vibrant green sine wave (#4a9e3d) positioned in the center-left area with high softness (0.62), creating a soft-edged, glowing appearance that dominates the composition. A secondary golden-tan sine wave (#c49a2e) is positioned in the upper-right quadrant with lower softness (0.42), creating a more defined edge. Both waves have animated motion with different speeds (0.3 and 0.5 respectively), creating a sense of fluid movement. The waves blend using OKLCH and OKLAB color spaces, producing subtle color transitions and gradients where they interact. A wave distortion effect with sine-type modulation (angle 52°, frequency 1.6) adds subtle rippling and warping across the entire composition, creating depth and movement. The overall effect is soft and atmospheric with a slight film grain overlay (strength 0.1) adding subtle texture and visual noise. The composition has a dreamy, ethereal quality with warm and cool color contrasts creating visual interest and depth.
import:
import {
  Shader,
  FilmGrain,
  SineWave,
  SolidColor,
  WaveDistortion
} from 'shaders/vue'
code:
<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  Shader,
  FilmGrain,
  SineWave,
  SolidColor,
  WaveDistortion
} from 'shaders/vue'
</script>

<template>
  <Shader>
    <SolidColor
      color="#0a0f12"/>
    <SineWave
      :amplitude="0.52"
      :angle="77"
      blend-mode="normal-oklch"
      color="#4a9e3d"
      :frequency="0.35"
      :position="{
        x: 0.38,
        y: 0.72
      }"
      :softness="0.62"
      :speed="0.3"
      :thickness="0.95"/>
    <SineWave
      :amplitude="0.29"
      :angle="144"
      blend-mode="normal-oklab"
      color="#c49a2e"
      :frequency="0.45"
      :position="{
        x: 0.72,
        y: 0.33
      }"
      :softness="0.42"
      :speed="0.5"
      :thickness="0.58"/>
    <WaveDistortion
      :angle="52"
      edges="mirror"
      :frequency="1.6"
      :speed="0.2"
      :strength="0.45"/>
    <FilmGrain
      :strength="0.1"/>
  </Shader>
</template>
usage:
The outer <Shader> component renders a <canvas> and needs explicit sizing. Typically it's positioned absolute behind content (e.g. inset: 0, position: absolute) as a background effect, or given explicit width/height. Apply style, class, and any layout attributes just like a normal HTML element.
SSR: Wrap with <ClientOnly> in Nuxt, or use a v-if="isMounted" guard. WebGPU requires the browser.
